description: One who holds special place in the heart, treasured above others. Beloved encompasses romantic love, deep affection, soul recognition, and the understanding that belovedness transforms both lover and loved.
rationale: The word 'whekani' begins with aspirated 'whe', strengthens through 'ka', and grounds in 'ni'. Using only a fricative digraph, this three-syllable journey captures belovedness as sacred breath building into grounded devotion.
